prediction:
FROM products p
| SELECT p.product_name, p.price
| ORDER p.price DESC
| LIMIT 2
repair_actions: Removed p.product_id from the SELECT projection so the output matches the question's likely intent (only product name and price).
Kept ORDER BY p.price DESC and LIMIT 2 unchanged to return the two highest-priced products.

prediction:
FROM customers c
| LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id AND o.status = 'paid'
| GROUP c.customer_id, c.customer_name
| AGG c.customer_id, c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS paid_order_count
repair_actions: Changed INNER JOIN to LEFT JOIN to include customers with zero paid orders.
Moved o.status = 'paid' filter from WHERE into the JOIN condition so LEFT JOIN rows without orders are not excluded.
Removed the WHERE clause to avoid filtering out customers with no matching paid orders.
Kept GROUP and AGG the same to produce per-customer paid order counts.

prediction:
FROM customers c
| GROUP c.level
| AGG c.level, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customer_count
| ORDER customer_count DESC, c.level ASC
repair_actions: Added ORDER clause to enforce deterministic ordering: ORDER customer_count DESC, c.level ASC (sort by count descending, tie-break by level ascending) because feedback indicated order_or_limit_suspected.

prediction:

prediction:

| JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
| WHERE o.status = 'paid'
| GROUP o.order_id
| AGG o.order_id, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS order_amount

prediction:
FROM products p
| JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
| JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
| WHERE o.status = 'paid'
| WHERE p.category = 'furniture'
| GROUP p.product_id, p.product_name
| AGG SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity
| SELECT p.product_name, total_quantity
repair_actions: Added GROUP by p.product_id and p.product_name to return per-product aggregates (tags included 'group').
Added SELECT to output product_name along with the aggregated total_quantity.

prediction:
FROM customers c
| LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id AND o.status = 'paid'
| LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
| LEFT JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
| GROUP c.customer_id, c.customer_name
| AGG c.customer_id, c.customer_name, COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS category_count
| ORDER category_count DESC
repair_actions: Change INNER JOINs to LEFT JOINs so customers without purchases are included (show zero counts).
Move the order status filter into the JOIN condition: add "AND o.status = 'paid'" to the orders join to count only paid purchases without turning the LEFT JOIN into an effective INNER JOIN.
Keep GROUP BY on c.customer_id, c.customer_name and COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) to compute distinct product categories per customer.
